summary:
The pulpal floor of an amalgam Class II preparation needs to be positioned deeper compared to an inlay due to enhanced retentive properties required by amalgam restorations; whereas inlays prioritize preserving more dental structure near the pulp horns. ``` Summary:

The pulpal floor for an amalgam Class II preparation should be placed deeper than for an inlay to ensure better retention and stability, while inlays preserve more coronal tooth structure closer to the pulp chambers.
```
